UI/UX Audit Report: OCBC Dual Currency Investment (DCI)

All Findings (22)

#Screen / ComponentDimensionSeverityFindingRecommendation
#1 T&C Modal 10. Trust & Emotional Design CRITICAL Terms & Conditions modal contains Lorem Ipsum placeholder text. Users are asked to click 'Agree' to accept nonsensical Latin text for a regulated financial product. Replace Lorem Ipsum with actual DCI Terms and Conditions covering: product risks, settlement mechanics, early termination policy, regulatory disclaimers, and dispute resolution.
#2 Investment Parameters / Review 10. Trust & Emotional Design CRITICAL No explicit risk warning or risk acknowledgment anywhere in the investment placement flow (Steps 1-4). The only risk information is buried in an FAQ accordion on the landing page. Users can commit $200,000 USD without ever being shown that they may receive a different currency at maturity. Add a dedicated risk disclosure panel on the Review screen (before Submit) with: (1) explicit statement 'You may receive the Alternate Currency if the fixing rate is below the strike price at expiry', (2) a mandatory risk acknowledgment checkbox, (3) link to Key Fact Sheet/term sheet.
#3 Investment Parameters (fully configured) 9. Cognitive Load & Clarity CRITICAL Scenario B (Alternate Currency settlement) does not show the equivalent value in the base currency. User sees '257,000.00 SGD' but cannot tell if this represents a gain or loss compared to their 200,000 USD investment. The actual USD-equivalent loss is hidden. Add a line to Scenario B showing the base-currency equivalent: 'Equivalent to ~196,923 USD (based on current spot rate)' with red text if it represents a loss. Also add a clear label: 'This scenario means you receive SGD instead of your original USD.'
#4 All screens with forms and text 8. Accessibility (WCAG 2.2) CRITICAL 9 distinct WCAG AA contrast failures identified: helper/description text (~2.8:1), 'Expiring Soon' badge (~3.0:1), table column headers (~2.8:1), 'indicative yield'/'from spot' labels (~2.8:1), form placeholders (~2.3:1), footer text (~2.8:1), timestamp text (~2.3:1), 'Alternate Currency' badge (~4.0:1). Required: 4.5:1 for normal text. Increase all secondary text to minimum #595959 on white backgrounds (4.5:1). Status badges should use darker background colors or larger text. Conduct a full color audit using a contrast checker tool.
#5 All screens 8. Accessibility (WCAG 2.2) CRITICAL Zero keyboard focus indicators are designed across all 29 screens. No focus rings on buttons, inputs, dropdowns, radio buttons, links, tabs, or modals. WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) is completely unmet. Add a 2px offset focus ring (e.g., blue #2563EB) to all interactive elements. Define focus order for the multi-step flow. Ensure the T&C modal traps focus correctly.
#6 Reinvest Step 1 (Strike NOT Met) 9. Cognitive Load & Clarity HIGH After 'Strike Price Not Met' settlement (user received SGD instead of USD), the reinvestment flow silently swaps the currency pair from USD/SGD to SGD/EUR with no explanation. The investment amount changes from USD to SGD. User may not understand why the base currency changed. Add an explanatory banner at the top of the reinvest screen: 'Your previous investment settled in SGD (Alternate Currency). You are now reinvesting your SGD proceeds.' Offer an option to convert back to the original base currency before reinvesting.
#7 All screens 1. Information Architecture & Navigation HIGH Three different navigation bar configurations appear across the DCI flow: (A) 'FX and Investment' during placement, (B) 'Trade finance' on the MCA prerequisite screen with different casing, (C) 'FX and Treasury' on investment detail screens. Users cannot form a consistent mental model of where they are. Standardise on one navigation label (recommend 'FX and Investment') across ALL DCI-related screens. Ensure consistent casing and active-state styling.
#8 Investment Parameters / Review 9. Cognitive Load & Clarity HIGH Yield is displayed as '6.50% p.a.' (per annum) on a 1-month investment, but the actual return for the period is only ~0.54%. No clarification is provided. The annualized figure is visually prominent (large, bold) while no actual-period return is shown. Show both values: '6.50% p.a. (~0.54% for 1 month)'. Add a tooltip or info icon explaining 'Annual yield is prorated for your selected tenor. Your estimated return for this period is $1,083.33 USD.'
#9 Investment Parameters (empty → fully configured) 6. Forms & Data Entry HIGH No step indicator or progress bar in the multi-step DCI placement flow (Parameters → Accounts → Review → Confirmation). User has no idea how many steps remain or where they are in the process. Add a horizontal stepper/progress indicator at the top of each step: 'Step 1 of 4: Investment Parameters → Step 2: Account Selection → Step 3: Review → Step 4: Confirmation'.
#10 Investment Parameters (fully configured) 6. Forms & Data Entry HIGH Strike price selection offers 5 options (e.g., 1.2830 to 1.2870) with '% from spot' and 'indicative yield' but no explanation of what strike price means or how to choose. A novice sees radio buttons with decimal numbers and percentages with zero guidance. Add: (1) An info icon with tooltip explaining 'Strike price is the exchange rate that determines which currency you receive. Higher strike = higher yield but higher chance of receiving the alternate currency.' (2) A visual indicator showing spot rate position relative to strike options. (3) Label the trade-off: 'Higher yield ↔ Higher conversion risk'.
#11 Investment Parameters (empty) 6. Forms & Data Entry MEDIUM Currency defaults to 'NIL' with no flag icon instead of a sensible default based on the user's account currencies. The 'Base Currency' and 'Alternate Currency' dropdowns show generic labels with no explanation of which is deposit vs. settlement currency. Default to the user's primary account currency (e.g., USD). Add helper text under the currency pair: 'Base Currency = the currency you deposit. Alternate Currency = the currency you may receive at maturity.'
#12 Investment Parameters 6. Forms & Data Entry MEDIUM The minimum amount helper text shows '$0,000.00 USD' which appears to be a formatting/template bug. Should display the actual minimum (e.g., '$50,000.00 USD'). Fix the template to display the actual minimum amount for the selected currency. Format consistently with thousand separators.
#13 Landing page (active investments) 4. Colour & Contrast MEDIUM Green is overloaded across 6 different semantic meanings: active investment status, positive yield values, success checkmark, base currency scenario badge, strike price met indicator, and yield percentages. Users cannot quickly scan for meaning when the same colour means different things. Differentiate semantic uses: keep green for success/positive only. Use blue for informational status (Active), use a distinct colour (e.g., teal) for base currency scenarios, and use a neutral colour for yield percentages.
#14 All form screens 8. Accessibility (WCAG 2.2) MEDIUM Form input borders (~#E0E0E0 on white) have an estimated contrast ratio of ~2.5:1, failing WCAG 1.4.11 (Non-text Contrast) which requires 3:1. Input fields are visually indistinct from the background. Darken input borders to at least #949494 (3:1 on white). Consider adding a subtle background fill (#F9F9F9) to inactive inputs.
#15 All screens 7. Feedback & System Status MEDIUM Zero error states and zero loading states are designed across the entire 29-screen flow. No designs for: insufficient balance, rate expiry during placement, network timeout, session expiry, or invalid input. Design error states for: (1) insufficient balance, (2) rate/quote expiry (with refresh option), (3) session timeout, (4) network error, (5) form validation errors (inline). Add loading/skeleton states for rate fetching and order submission.
#16 Review 5. Component & Interaction Design MEDIUM The 'REVIEW' label is positioned in the far-left margin, visually disconnected from the main content. Combined with no step indicator, the user's current position in the flow is unclear. The label does not follow the same visual pattern as other page titles. Replace with a proper step indicator showing 'Step 3 of 4: Review Your Investment'. Position as a page heading within the main content area.
#17 Landing page (active investments) 5. Component & Interaction Design MEDIUM Status badges are inconsistent: 'Active' uses text-only styling, 'Expiring Soon' uses amber text, 'Awaiting Settlement' uses a pill badge with background colour. No unified design language for investment statuses. Standardise all statuses as pill badges with consistent sizing: Active (green), Expiring Soon (amber), Awaiting Settlement (blue), Settled (gray). Use the same component for all.
#18 Landing page 9. Cognitive Load & Clarity MEDIUM The 4 key DCI dates (Transaction, Value, Expiry, Maturity) use different labels across screens. The FAQ uses 'Trade Date' while the product flow uses 'Transaction Date'. The Investment Timeline explains each date but the explanation only appears in the parameters step, not on the investment details screen. Standardise date labels across all screens and FAQs. Include the Investment Timeline explanation on the Investment Details screen as well, not just during placement.
#19 Confirmation (collapsed) 1. Information Architecture & Navigation MEDIUM The confirmation screen shows only 'Place Another Investment' as the next action. There is no link to 'View My Portfolio', 'View Investment Details', or 'Return to Dashboard'. The user is stranded after placing a $200,000 investment. Add secondary actions: 'View Investment Details' (link to the new investment) and 'Go to Portfolio' (link back to the landing page). Keep 'Place Another Investment' as the secondary CTA.
#20 Investment Parameters 6. Forms & Data Entry MEDIUM No inline validation is visible in any form screen. Users can only discover errors at submission time. The 'Next' button appears always enabled regardless of form completion state. Add inline validation for: minimum amount, valid currency pair combinations, tenor availability. Disable the Next button until required fields are completed. Show validation messages in real-time.
#21 Investment Details (Expiring Soon) 7. Feedback & System Status LOW The 'Expiring Soon' status shows 'Fixing Rate: Determined on 10 Nov 2025 (2PM, SGT)' but provides no countdown, no notification preference, and no action the user can take. The screen is purely informational with only a 'Back' button. Add: (1) a countdown to fixing date, (2) 'Set a reminder' option, (3) explanation of what happens after fixing. Consider showing the live spot rate vs strike price so users can estimate their likely outcome.
#22 Landing page 2. Visual Hierarchy & Layout LOW The value proposition cards ('Maximise your investment returns' and 'Great for currency conversion needs') use small text and generic icons that do not visually differentiate from each other. Both look identical in weight and importance. Use distinct, illustrative icons for each value prop. Increase heading font size. Consider adding a short 'How it works' visual (3-step diagram) that shows the DCI lifecycle: Invest → Wait for Expiry → Receive Settlement.

Annotated Screenshots

Each screenshot is annotated with numbered markers corresponding to findings above. Markers are colour-coded by severity.

Day 1 Add Delete Users 3

Findings on this screen:

#2 CRITICAL No explicit risk warning or risk acknowledgment anywhere in the investment placement flow (Steps 1-4). The only risk information is buried in an FAQ accordion on the landing page. Users can commit $200,000 USD without ever being shown that they may receive a different currency at maturity.
→ Add a dedicated risk disclosure panel on the Review screen (before Submit) with: (1) explicit statement 'You may receive the Alternate Currency if the fixing rate is below the strike price at expiry', (2) a mandatory risk acknowledgment checkbox, (3) link to Key Fact Sheet/term sheet.
#3 CRITICAL Scenario B (Alternate Currency settlement) does not show the equivalent value in the base currency. User sees '257,000.00 SGD' but cannot tell if this represents a gain or loss compared to their 200,000 USD investment. The actual USD-equivalent loss is hidden.
→ Add a line to Scenario B showing the base-currency equivalent: 'Equivalent to ~196,923 USD (based on current spot rate)' with red text if it represents a loss. Also add a clear label: 'This scenario means you receive SGD instead of your original USD.'
#8 HIGH Yield is displayed as '6.50% p.a.' (per annum) on a 1-month investment, but the actual return for the period is only ~0.54%. No clarification is provided. The annualized figure is visually prominent (large, bold) while no actual-period return is shown.
→ Show both values: '6.50% p.a. (~0.54% for 1 month)'. Add a tooltip or info icon explaining 'Annual yield is prorated for your selected tenor. Your estimated return for this period is $1,083.33 USD.'
#10 HIGH Strike price selection offers 5 options (e.g., 1.2830 to 1.2870) with '% from spot' and 'indicative yield' but no explanation of what strike price means or how to choose. A novice sees radio buttons with decimal numbers and percentages with zero guidance.
→ Add: (1) An info icon with tooltip explaining 'Strike price is the exchange rate that determines which currency you receive. Higher strike = higher yield but higher chance of receiving the alternate currency.' (2) A visual indicator showing spot rate position relative to strike options. (3) Label the trade-off: 'Higher yield ↔ Higher conversion risk'.
#9 HIGH No step indicator or progress bar in the multi-step DCI placement flow (Parameters → Accounts → Review → Confirmation). User has no idea how many steps remain or where they are in the process.
→ Add a horizontal stepper/progress indicator at the top of each step: 'Step 1 of 4: Investment Parameters → Step 2: Account Selection → Step 3: Review → Step 4: Confirmation'.
#11 MEDIUM Currency defaults to 'NIL' with no flag icon instead of a sensible default based on the user's account currencies. The 'Base Currency' and 'Alternate Currency' dropdowns show generic labels with no explanation of which is deposit vs. settlement currency.
→ Default to the user's primary account currency (e.g., USD). Add helper text under the currency pair: 'Base Currency = the currency you deposit. Alternate Currency = the currency you may receive at maturity.'
#18 MEDIUM The 4 key DCI dates (Transaction, Value, Expiry, Maturity) use different labels across screens. The FAQ uses 'Trade Date' while the product flow uses 'Transaction Date'. The Investment Timeline explains each date but the explanation only appears in the parameters step, not on the investment details screen.
→ Standardise date labels across all screens and FAQs. Include the Investment Timeline explanation on the Investment Details screen as well, not just during placement.

Overseas Confirmation Maker Collapsed

Findings on this screen:

#19 MEDIUM The confirmation screen shows only 'Place Another Investment' as the next action. There is no link to 'View My Portfolio', 'View Investment Details', or 'Return to Dashboard'. The user is stranded after placing a $200,000 investment.
→ Add secondary actions: 'View Investment Details' (link to the new investment) and 'Go to Portfolio' (link back to the landing page). Keep 'Place Another Investment' as the secondary CTA.

Reinvest Step 1 Strike Price Not Met

Findings on this screen:

#6 HIGH After 'Strike Price Not Met' settlement (user received SGD instead of USD), the reinvestment flow silently swaps the currency pair from USD/SGD to SGD/EUR with no explanation. The investment amount changes from USD to SGD. User may not understand why the base currency changed.
→ Add an explanatory banner at the top of the reinvest screen: 'Your previous investment settled in SGD (Alternate Currency). You are now reinvesting your SGD proceeds.' Offer an option to convert back to the original base currency before reinvesting.

Findings on this screen:

#7 HIGH Three different navigation bar configurations appear across the DCI flow: (A) 'FX and Investment' during placement, (B) 'Trade finance' on the MCA prerequisite screen with different casing, (C) 'FX and Treasury' on investment detail screens. Users cannot form a consistent mental model of where they are.
→ Standardise on one navigation label (recommend 'FX and Investment') across ALL DCI-related screens. Ensure consistent casing and active-state styling.
#13 MEDIUM Green is overloaded across 6 different semantic meanings: active investment status, positive yield values, success checkmark, base currency scenario badge, strike price met indicator, and yield percentages. Users cannot quickly scan for meaning when the same colour means different things.
→ Differentiate semantic uses: keep green for success/positive only. Use blue for informational status (Active), use a distinct colour (e.g., teal) for base currency scenarios, and use a neutral colour for yield percentages.
#17 MEDIUM Status badges are inconsistent: 'Active' uses text-only styling, 'Expiring Soon' uses amber text, 'Awaiting Settlement' uses a pill badge with background colour. No unified design language for investment statuses.
→ Standardise all statuses as pill badges with consistent sizing: Active (green), Expiring Soon (amber), Awaiting Settlement (blue), Settled (gray). Use the same component for all.
#22 LOW The value proposition cards ('Maximise your investment returns' and 'Great for currency conversion needs') use small text and generic icons that do not visually differentiate from each other. Both look identical in weight and importance.
→ Use distinct, illustrative icons for each value prop. Increase heading font size. Consider adding a short 'How it works' visual (3-step diagram) that shows the DCI lifecycle: Invest → Wait for Expiry → Receive Settlement.

Review

Findings on this screen:

#2 CRITICAL No explicit risk warning or risk acknowledgment anywhere in the investment placement flow (Steps 1-4). The only risk information is buried in an FAQ accordion on the landing page. Users can commit $200,000 USD without ever being shown that they may receive a different currency at maturity.
→ Add a dedicated risk disclosure panel on the Review screen (before Submit) with: (1) explicit statement 'You may receive the Alternate Currency if the fixing rate is below the strike price at expiry', (2) a mandatory risk acknowledgment checkbox, (3) link to Key Fact Sheet/term sheet.
#4 CRITICAL 9 distinct WCAG AA contrast failures identified: helper/description text (~2.8:1), 'Expiring Soon' badge (~3.0:1), table column headers (~2.8:1), 'indicative yield'/'from spot' labels (~2.8:1), form placeholders (~2.3:1), footer text (~2.8:1), timestamp text (~2.3:1), 'Alternate Currency' badge (~4.0:1). Required: 4.5:1 for normal text.
→ Increase all secondary text to minimum #595959 on white backgrounds (4.5:1). Status badges should use darker background colors or larger text. Conduct a full color audit using a contrast checker tool.
#16 MEDIUM The 'REVIEW' label is positioned in the far-left margin, visually disconnected from the main content. Combined with no step indicator, the user's current position in the flow is unclear. The label does not follow the same visual pattern as other page titles.
→ Replace with a proper step indicator showing 'Step 3 of 4: Review Your Investment'. Position as a page heading within the main content area.
#20 MEDIUM No inline validation is visible in any form screen. Users can only discover errors at submission time. The 'Next' button appears always enabled regardless of form completion state.
→ Add inline validation for: minimum amount, valid currency pair combinations, tenor availability. Disable the Next button until required fields are completed. Show validation messages in real-time.
